prevalence.trend: Stable to slightly increasing due to aging population and hypertension prevalence

incidence.source: Ministério da Saúde/DataSUS (2023 hospital admissions data)

burden.source: Global Burden of Disease Study 2021 (IHME) adapted for Brazil

vulnerablePopulations: Low socioeconomic status groups (higher in Northeast region)
Indigenous and rural populations (limited access to care)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical symptoms (sudden focal neurological deficit) confirmed by neuroimaging (CT/MRI); subtypes: ischemic (85%, due to thrombosis/embolism) vs. hemorrhagic (15%, intracerebral/subarachnoid); scales like NIHSS for severity

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: 25% for hypertension screening in primary care (SUS data)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Hypertension control: 45%; thrombolysis access: 10% within golden hour

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Follows AHA/ASA guidelines adapted by SBC; emphasis on rapid CT availability in SUS hospitals
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: SUS protocol includes rt-PA for ischemic within 4.5 hours; antiplatelets/anticoagulants; hemorrhagic management with neurosurgery where available
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Regional disparities (South/Southeast better access; North/Northeast higher mortality due to delayed care); infrastructure gaps in rural areas
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Universal SUS coverage; national stroke registry initiatives; growing telestroke networks in urban centers
